articles.title: 'If you're not close enough, your photo is not good enough': Remembering Raghu Rai
articles.description: Legendary photographer Raghu Rai, who shaped India's visual memory for over five decades, passed away on Sunday. His iconic images, from the Bhopal Gas tragedy to portraits of national figures, gave permanence to history. Rai believed visual history was more important than fine art, emphasizing closeness to truth in his immersive photographic practice.

articles.title: Militants and separatists launch coordinated attacks across Mali
articles.description:
Al-Qaida-linked group JNIM claims responsibility for strikes on airport in capital, Bamako and four other cities
Islamic militants and separatists attacked several locations in Mali’s capital and other cities on Saturday in one of the largest coordinated attacks in the country in recent years.
The al-Qaida-linked militant group JNIM claimed responsibility for the attacks on Bamako’s international airport and four other cities in central and northern Mali on its website, Az-Zallaqa. It said the attacks were carried out jointly with the Azawad Liberation Front, a Tuareg-led separatist group.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Trump fires the entire National Science Board
articles.description:
Multiple sources are reporting that the Trump administration has dismissed the entire National Science Board (NSB). The NSB advises the president and Congress on the National Science Foundation (NSF), which has already been funding research at historically low levels and has seen significant delays in doling out that funding. The NSF has been fundamental in helping develop technology used in MRIs, cellphones, and it even helped get Duolingo get off the ground.
 In a statement, Zoe Lofgren, the ranking Democrat on the House Science, Space, and Technology Committee, said:
"This is the latest stupid move made by a president who continues to  …

Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: An influx of used EVs could drive down prices
articles.description:
Part of what has held back electric cars has been the cost. But an influx of used vehicles over the next three years could bring prices down dramatically. In 2025, just 123,000 leases on EVs expired. That is expected to more than double to 300,000 in 2026, and double again to 600,000 in 2027 and 660,000 in 2028, according to Cox Automotive. 
Most leased vehicles end up entering the used market. This means more than a million used EVs could become available over the next few years, making them far more accessible. The vast majority of cars sold in the US are used - some 76 percent as of 2024, according to Consumer Affairs. A large part of th …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Illegal sex determination tests in Mandya: DC issues directions for inspections of all scanning centres in district
articles.description: The lapses by the private diagnostic centres including their failure to maintain proper records of the tests conducted by them had been shared with the officials

articles.title: PM Modi hails Kalpakkam nuclear reactor in Mann Ki Baat, urges Census participation
articles.description: In his 133rd Mann Ki Baat, PM Modi calls Kalpakkam fast breeder reactor’s first criticality a historic milestone and urges participation in Census 2027.

articles.title: King Charles U.S. visit comes at tense moment in transatlantic relationship
articles.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive Monday for a four day U.S. state visit. Some hope the royal touch can heal the transatlantic rift that's emerged under Trump.

articles.title: Ikea's New Blow-Up Chair Was Tested by Cats
articles.description: Part of the new PS 2026 collection, it's taken the Swedish company 26 years to finally nail inflatable furniture.

articles.title: Raghav Chadha, six AAP Rajya Sabha MPs quit party, set to merge with BJP
articles.description: MPs Raghav Chadha, Sandeep Pathak, and Ashok Kumar Mittal met BJP president Nitin Nabin at the party’s headquarters

articles.title: Azim Premji University to host Sahitya Sahavasa in Mysuru on April 28
articles.description: The day-long programme to be inaugurated by former Vice Chancellor of Sanskrit University Dr. Padma Shekar will celebrate the literary contributions of eminent Kannada writers

topStories.title: King Charles U.S. visit comes at tense moment in transatlantic relationship
topStories.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive Monday for a four day U.S. state visit. Some hope the royal touch can heal the transatlantic rift that's emerged under Trump.

topStories.title: Dad of illegal immigrant DUI victim issues warning to Americans over lax driver's license laws
topStories.description: Joe Abraham warns Americans after his daughter was killed by an illegal immigrant drunken driver, urging stricter driver's license laws and proper vetting.

topStories.title: In pictures: Before and after the 2026 White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting
topStories.description: From arrivals to evacuation, see photos from the 2026 White House Correspondents’ Dinner after a shooting halted the night.

topStories.title: Israel’s President, Putting Off Decision on Pardon for Netanyahu, Will Push for Plea Deal
topStories.description: President Isaac Herzog of Israel has decided not to issue a pardon to Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in his corruption case at this time, and instead will seek mediation, officials say.

topStories.title: What to know about Cole Allen, alleged WH correspondents’ dinner shooter
topStories.description: Donald Trump evacuated from stage after firing at media gala; suspect Cole Tomas Allen in custody.

topStories.title: U.S. Sanctions Zigzag in New World of Economic Warfare
topStories.description: With oil prices in mind, the Trump administration has deployed a haphazard approach to sanctions on Russia and Iran.

topStories.title: California DOGE leader slams Newsom, Bonta over state’s massive fraud issues: “Every day is opposite day"
topStories.description: CAL DOGE director Jenny Rae Le Roux slams Gov. Newsom and AG Bonta, alleging they failed to crack down on widespread fraud across California programs.

topStories.title: They left Congress in disgrace. Taxpayers could still pay their pensions.
topStories.description: To toughen how Congress polices itself, a bill would prohibit members from receiving pensions if they are convicted of crimes including rape and sexual assault.

topStories.title: Trump, allies use dinner shooting to press case for White House ballroom
topStories.description: A White House ballroom would be far more secure than any hotel facility, Trump says, as he and his allies argue that Saturday’s shooting bolsters their case.

topStories.title: The Supreme Court case that could redefine your digital privacy
topStories.description: Police in Virginia used a technique called geofencing to tap into Google's databases to find out who was near the scene of a bank robbery. The Supreme Court will consider whether it is constitutional.

topStories.title: Journalists' Posts From Trump Event Spark 'Safe Or Unsafe Venue' Debate

topStories.title: What we know about the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
topStories.description: An armed man exchanged gunfire with law enforcement after he rushed a security checkpoint at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in Washington, D.C. on Saturday, authorities said, an event attended by President Donald Trump and high-ranking members of his administration.

dailyNews.title: What we know about the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
dailyNews.description: An armed man exchanged gunfire with law enforcement after he rushed a security checkpoint at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in Washington, D.C. on Saturday, authorities said, an event attended by President Donald Trump and high-ranking members of his administration.

dailyNews.title: Trump safe after shots fired at White House Correspondents' Dinner; suspect in custody
dailyNews.description: President Trump was safely evacuated from the White House Correspondents' Dinner Saturday night after shots were fired outside the ballroom of the Washington Hilton Hotel.

dailyNews.title: WHCA dinner shooting live updates: Suspect armed with multiple guns and knives
dailyNews.description: A suspect is in custody following a shooting incident Saturday at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner at the Washington, D.C. Hilton hotel.

dailyNews.title: Suspect in White House Correspondents' Association dinner shooting identified
dailyNews.description: The man suspected of opening fire at officers during the White House Correspondents' Association dinner in Washington, D.C., on Saturday is a 31-year-old from Southern California, authorities said

dailyNews.title: Trump: Shooter at White House press dinner had multiple weapons, one officer shot
dailyNews.description: Trump, seated next to first lady Melania Trump, was quickly surrounded by Secret Service agents, while many attendees ducked under their tables.

dailyNews.title: ‘He has the market in a chokehold’: Stocks swing as Trump posts
dailyNews.description: US stock market moves over the past 15 months have been heavily influenced by President Donald Trump’s remarks and social media posts. Fundstrat analysis shows his comments drove the S&P 500’s biggest gains and losses, an influence unmatched by any modern US leader. Analysts say this level of market impact is unprecedented and highlights investors’ sensitivity to political signals.

dailyNews.title: Mississippi middle schoolers stop runaway bus after driver loses consciousness from asthma attack
dailyNews.description: Dozens of Mississippi middle school students sprang into action to stop a school bus after their driver suffered an asthma attack and lost consciousness.

dailyNews.title: DNA found on a smoothie straw links suspect to teen's 1984 killing
dailyNews.description: After more than 40 years and three wrongful convictions, authorities says they have the man responsible for the 1984 Long Island killing of 16-year-old Theresa Fusco, who vanished after leaving her job at a local roller rink.

dailyNews.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
dailyNews.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

dailyNews.title: The Killing of Theresa Fusco
dailyNews.description: Nearly 41 years after a New York teenager is killed, an unexpected breakthrough in the case.  "48 Hours" correspondent Erin Moriarty reports.

dailyNews.title: What we know about the suspect in shooting at White House press dinner
dailyNews.description: The suspect was identified to CBS News by law enforcement sources as 31-year-old Cole Allen of Torrance, California.

dailyNews.title: Trump says "it's a dangerous profession" after shots fired at dinner
dailyNews.description: President Trump was evacuated from the White House Correspondents' Dinner after shots rang out at the hotel where the event was taking place.

dailyNews.description: Very little was clear about what had happened at the Washington Hilton on Saturday night. But the president wanted to talk about it.

dailyNews.title: Inside the chaotic correspondents’ dinner shooting: ‘Get down, get down!’
dailyNews.description: For the third time in three years, President Donald Trump found himself under the threat of gunfire. This time the Washington press corps was in the room.

dailyNews.title: Trump says he sees shootings as a reflection of his impact
dailyNews.description: “I hate to say I’m honored,” the president says of his repeated brushes with violence, including two assassination attempts during his 2024 campaign.

dailyNews.title: Correspondents’ dinner chaos hits high-profile guests already marked by political violence
dailyNews.description: Trump spoke about repeated assassination attempts after chaos broke out Saturday, with RFK Jr. and Steve Scalise among those escorted from the room.

dailyNews.title: California Man in Custody After Shooting at Dinner Attended by Trump
dailyNews.description: Shots were fired at the hotel hosting the White House Correspondents Dinner. Authorities said the attack was carried out by a lone gunman who was brought down by Secret Service.
